turns.speaker: D
turns.text: hi , cheryl . how is cheryl ?

turns.speaker: P
turns.text: cheryl is doing well . how is the doctor ?

turns.speaker: D
turns.text: the doctor is doing well . so the doctor knows the nurse told cheryl a little bit about dax . the doctor would like to tell dax about cheryl .

turns.speaker: P
turns.text: okay .

turns.speaker: D
turns.text: cheryl is a 34-year-old female with a past medical history significant for hypertension , who presents today with back pain . cheryl , what happened to cheryl's back ?

turns.speaker: P
turns.text: so cheryl has been walking a lot lately . cheryl has been walking to ... 30 minutes to an hour or so a day . and all of a sudden , um , when cheryl was walking , cheryl's , um , back just kind of seized up on cheryl . and cheryl do n't really know what the back pain episode was . maybe cheryl was going a little bit faster . but cheryl's back just all kind of clenched .

turns.speaker: D
turns.text: okay . so the doctor felt like cheryl had , maybe like a spasm or something like a spasm ?

turns.speaker: P
turns.text: yeah .

turns.speaker: D
turns.text: okay . and how many days ago was the back pain episode ?

turns.speaker: P
turns.text: the back pain episode was about six days ago now .

turns.speaker: D
turns.text: okay . and what has cheryl taken for the back pain ?

turns.speaker: P
turns.text: cheryl has been taking ibuprofen . um , and then cheryl has been putting some heat on cheryl's back . but cheryl's back is still pretty stiff .

turns.speaker: D
turns.text: okay . all right . um , and did cheryl have any trauma before the back pain episode happened ? was cheryl doing anything strenuous like crossfit or lifting boxes or anything like that before cheryl went for , for the walk ?

turns.speaker: P
turns.text: cheryl have been lifting more , um , probably around three times a week . so cheryl do n't know if the back pain episode was because cheryl was doing deadlifts that day and then walked .

turns.speaker: D
turns.text: okay .

turns.speaker: P
turns.text: um , maybe cheryl was using cheryl's back more than cheryl's legs .

turns.speaker: D
turns.text: okay . all right . and was the back pain in any particular area in cheryl's back ? was the back pain in the lower back ?

turns.speaker: P
turns.text: yeah , the back pain was .

turns.speaker: D
turns.text: okay . on one side versus the other side ?

turns.speaker: P
turns.text: um , kind of both sides equally .

turns.speaker: D
turns.text: okay . all right . and any numbing or tingling in cheryl's legs or cheryl's feet ?

turns.speaker: P
turns.text: no , cheryl have n't felt anything like numbing or tingling in cheryl's legs or cheryl's feet .

turns.speaker: D
turns.text: okay . any weakness in cheryl's lower extremities ?

turns.speaker: P
turns.text: no .

turns.speaker: D
turns.text: okay . all right . and then in terms of cheryl's blood pressure , how is cheryl doing ?

turns.speaker: P
turns.text: so cheryl got the blood pressure cuff that the doctor suggested the ... our ... the last visit , and cheryl has been doing blood pressure readings at home . and the blood pressure readings at home have been looking great , too . cheryl has been watching cheryl's diet . again , cheryl's boyfriend's been great and dieting with cheryl so cheryl do n't have to do the diet alone . and everything's been good .

turns.speaker: D
turns.text: okay . excellent . and is cheryl taking the lisinopril ?

turns.speaker: P
turns.text: yes .

turns.speaker: D
turns.text: okay . wonderful . okay . so the doctor knows cheryl did a review of systems sheet with the nurse , and the doctor knows cheryl endorse , cheryl know , this back pain . um , does cheryl have any other symptoms ? fever , chills , congestion , cough , chest pain , shortness of breath ?

turns.speaker: P
turns.text: cheryl have a little bit of nasal congestion , but the nasal congestion is just from cheryl's seasonal allergies .

turns.speaker: D
turns.text: okay . all right . well , let's go ahead . the doctor want to do a quick physical exam on cheryl .

turns.speaker: P
turns.text: okay .

turns.speaker: D
turns.text: okay ? hey , dragon , show the doctor the vital signs . so good- cheryl know , here in the office , cheryl's vital signs look great . cheryl's blood pressure's really well controlled , which is good . so that's a good job . so the doctor is going to take a listen to cheryl's heart and lungs . the doctor is going to examine cheryl's back , and the doctor is going to let cheryl know what the doctor find . okay ?

turns.speaker: P
turns.text: okay .

turns.speaker: D
turns.text: okay . all right . so on physical examination , cheryl know , everything looks good . cheryl know , on cheryl's heart exam , the doctor do hear that slight two out of six systolic ejection murmur , but cheryl've had that slight two out of six systolic ejection murmur before . that slight two out of six systolic ejection murmur seems stable to the doctor . on cheryl's back exam , cheryl do have some pain to palpation on the right lateral aspect of cheryl's lumbar spine , and cheryl do have pain with flexion and extension as well , and cheryl have a negative straight leg raise . so what does the physical examination mean ? so the doctor and cheryl're going to go over the physical examination . okay ? let's ... let the doctor look at some of cheryl's results , though , first . okay ?

turns.speaker: P
turns.text: okay .

turns.speaker: D
turns.text: the doctor and cheryl did an x-ray before cheryl saw the doctor , so let's look at the x-ray . hey , dragon , show the doctor the back x-ray . so looking here at this x-ray of the lumbar spine , everything looks good . there's good boney alignment . there's no obvious fracture , cheryl know , which is not surprising based on cheryl's history . okay ?

turns.speaker: P
turns.text: hmm .

turns.speaker: D
turns.text: hey , dragon , show the doctor the labs . and cheryl's labs that the doctor and cheryl did before cheryl came in all look great . there's no elevated white blood cell count . there's no signs of infection . again , the lab results are all really good . okay ? so let the doctor go over with cheryl about the doctor's assessment and the doctor's plan for cheryl . so for cheryl's first problem , this back pain , the doctor think cheryl have a lumbar strain , and , cheryl know , the lumbar strain might've happened , cheryl know , lifting something or exercising . and so what the doctor want to do is prescribe meloxicam , 15 milligrams once a day . uh , the doctor want cheryl ... cheryl can ice the area , and cheryl can also apply heat sometimes as well . um , cheryl know , the doctor is going to refer cheryl to physical therapy just to do some strengthening exercises of cheryl's back , um , because the doctor do want cheryl to continue to be able to work out and exercise . okay ?

turns.speaker: P
turns.text: okay .

turns.speaker: D
turns.text: and for cheryl's last problem , cheryl's high blood pressure , again , everything looks great here . um , cheryl know , the doctor think cheryl're doing a really good job with cheryl's high blood pressure as well . the doctor want cheryl to continue on the lisinopril , 10 milligrams a day . and then , uh , let the doctor know if cheryl notice any increases in cheryl's blood pressure readings . okay ?

turns.speaker: P
turns.text: okay .

turns.speaker: D
turns.text: does cheryl need a refill of the lisinopril ?

turns.speaker: P
turns.text: yes , cheryl do , actually .

turns.speaker: D
turns.text: okay . hey , dragon ? order lisinopril 10 milligrams po daily . okay . uh , so the nurse will be in soon , and the nurse'll get cheryl checked out . okay ?

turns.speaker: P
turns.text: okay .

turns.speaker: D
turns.text: all right . hey , dragon ? finalize the note .
